name: Pendulum dowsing ob3ect: a weighted pendulum suspended from a chain, used to answer yes/no questions through unconscious micromovements. FSPLIT: the dowser asks a question, splitting their own unconscious knowledge into directional signals — the pendulum's swing encodes what the conscious mind cannot access. FFUSE: the dowser interprets the swing pattern, reconstituting the unconscious signal into conscious answer. VINIT: the still pendulum. TANCH: the full arc of possible swing. AFWD: clockwise swing (yes/affirmation). AREV: counterclockwise swing (no/negation). ENGAGR: the pendulum oscillates in a straight line that is simultaneously clockwise and counterclockwise when viewed from opposite sides — the neutral/ambiguous answer.

phase_0.domain_name: Pendulum dowsing ob3ect: a weighted pendulum suspended from a chain, used to answer yes/no questions through unconscious micromovements. FSPLIT: the dowser asks a question, splitting their own unconscious knowledge into directional signals — the pendulum's swing encodes what the conscious mind cannot access. FFUSE: the dowser interprets the swing pattern, reconstituting the unconscious signal into conscious answer. VINIT: the still pendulum. TANCH: the full arc of possible swing. AFWD: clockwise swing (yes/affirmation). AREV: counterclockwise swing (no/negation). ENGAGR: the pendulum oscillates in a straight line that is simultaneously clockwise and counterclockwise when viewed from opposite sides — the neutral/ambiguous answer.
phase_0.boundary_condition: the full arc of possible swing

VINIT.justification: void/uninitialized state before any question is asked

TANCH.justification: terminal anchor that contains all possible swing directions

CLINK.justification: composition linking the dowser's hand to the pendulum's motion

IMSCRIB.justification: self-recognition that interprets the pendulum's signal

FSPLIT.justification: splits into directional signals: clockwise and counterclockwise swing patterns

FFUSE.justification: reconstitutes the two swing patterns into a single conscious answer

ENGAGR.justification: paradox: simultaneously clockwise and counterclockwise from opposite views, neutral answer

IFIX.justification: permanent irreversible fixation of the interpreted answer

steps.domain_action: Step 1 IMSCRIB: the dowser recognizes their own intent to ask a question
Step 2 AREV: the dowser descends into unconscious knowledge by focusing on the question
Step 3 FSPLIT: the unconscious knowledge splits into clockwise and counterclockwise swing patterns
Step 4 AFWD: the pendulum swings clockwise (affirmative signal) or counterclockwise (negative signal)
Step 5 FFUSE: the dowser interprets the swing pattern back into a conscious answer
Step 6 CLINK: the chain links the dowser's hand motion to the pendulum's swing, composing the answer
Step 7 IFIX: the answer is recorded permanently in memory
Step 8 IMSCRIB: the dowser recognizes the answer as their own

phase_5.memory_mechanism: the dowser's memory of past answers
phase_5.alfs_store: the still pendulum as bootstrap store

phase_6.cycle_cost: kinetic energy of the pendulum swing
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the system cycles between potential and kinetic energy without net dissipation
